subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV 2026, OECD, RSF, V-Dem, Freedom House) + Israeli official (CBS, Bank of Israel) + Israeli independent press (Haaretz, Times of Israel, Calcalist) + international, scored on observable actions/data.

Contested subject: anti- and pro-Israel framing were balanced by anchoring on IMF/Bank of Israel/CBS and the combative domestic press; conflict was credited for 2026 ceasefires rather than scored at active-war extremes.

Standout features: a world-class tech economy and a young, growing population, against institutional erosion (the judicial-overhaul crisis), high-variance multi-front conflict, and growing international isolation.

Recalibrated 2026-05-29 for the 2026 Iran war (Operation Epic Fury, US+Israel vs Iran from 28 Feb) and the Strait of Hormuz closure: blockade of ~1/5 of world oil+LNG, attacks/threats on Gulf desalination (water security), and the global energy shock. The two-week 8 Apr ceasefire collapsed by late May - the US renewed strikes on Iran ~25-26 May (Trump convened Camp David) and the war resumed, with Hormuz still blockaded. (Reuters; BBC; NYT; CNN; ISW; Al Jazeera; CSIS; Wikipedia)

economic.notes: A world-class tech-export base, a credible central bank, and sound banks, against war-widened deficits.

fiscal_state.notes: Deficit consolidating (6.8% in 2024 toward ~4.7% in 2025), debt rising from a low base toward ~69%, with 2024 rating downgrades. (IMF 2026 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: The Bank of Israel is credible and independent (rate cut to 3.75% in 2026), with a strengthening shekel. (Bank of Israel)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The shekel is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: ~1.9% and within the 1-3% band for months, with a war supply-blip managed. (Bank of Israel)

banking_stability.notes: High capital ratios, low arrears, record bank profits, and sound supervision. (Bank of Israel)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A world-class high-tech export base (the majority of exports, with record 2025 tech deals), against a reservist-mobilisation drag and boycott/isolation pressure; Leviathan gas, no resource curse. (Innovation Authority; Calcalist)

institutional.notes: Credible statistics and high state capacity, against an eroding rule-of-law trajectory and wartime press restrictions.

rule_of_law.notes: Historically strong courts, but a 2025 judicial-selection law politicises appointments and 2026 bills subordinate legal advisers, alongside the prime minister's corruption trial - eroding. (IDI; Times of Israel)

statistical_integrity.notes: The CBS is credible, with IMF/OECD reliance. (IMF Art IV; CBS)

civil_service_capacity.notes: High professional capacity, under politicisation pressure on appointments. (IDI; OECD)

press_freedom.notes: RSF ~112-116/180: an Al Jazeera ban, a foreign-press Gaza blackout, and wartime censorship, against a still-pluralist and combative domestic press. (RSF; CNN)

political_social.notes: A young, growing population, against deep polarisation, war trauma, and a divided society - near-term volatile with a settling upside.

political_stability.notes: Deeply polarised under a far-right coalition, with ceasefire/hostage crises, mass protests, and an election due in 2026. (Times of Israel; CFR)

civil_liberties.notes: Strong for Jewish citizens, with wartime erosion and differential treatment of Arab citizens and Palestinians. (V-Dem/Freedom House; Haaretz)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: The Law of Return gives Jewish immigrants near-automatic citizenship - uniquely open for that group - while remaining restrictive and hostile to non-Jewish migrants and asylum-seekers. (statute)

social_cohesion.notes: Deep secular/religious and Jewish-Arab cleavages compounded by war trauma. (IDI; Haaretz)

demographics.notes: High fertility (~2.8-3.0) and a young, growing population, with a Haredi labour-participation drag. (CBS; Taub Center)

geopolitical.notes: A deep US alliance and post-peak conflict with normalisation upside, against high relapse risk and growing isolation - high variance.

alliance_reliability.notes: US co-belligerency in the 2026 Iran war confirms a deep alliance, set against intensifying ICJ/ICC action, arms-embargo moves, and international isolation. (Times of Israel; CFR)

conflict_involvement.notes: The 2026 US-Israel war on Iran (Operation Epic Fury, from 28 Feb): multi-front strikes across Iran, Hezbollah/Lebanon and Iraq, with ~35 Israeli dead and ~8,600 injured. The two-week 8 Apr ceasefire collapsed by late May (US renewed strikes ~25-26 May); the war has resumed and peace talks are in crisis. Long horizon still holds a normalisation tail if a settlement is eventually reached. (CNN; ISW; Al Jazeera)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An open capital account, with external ICJ/ICC and boycott/embargo pressure rather than domestic controls. (ICJ; Times of Israel)

physical_practical.notes: Tech-managed water and strong healthcare and infrastructure, against a security duality and war/rocket exposure.

climate.notes: High readiness (desal, water tech) buffers Eastern-Med warming, drought, and heatwaves, with persistent coastal/aridification pressure. (IPCC AR6; ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: Universal coverage and strong outcomes, war-strained. (OECD; CBS)

infrastructure.notes: Advanced, with near-term rocket/missile exposure and war damage. (INSS)

crime_safety.notes: Low general street crime, but a record Arab-sector homicide surge (with very low clearance) and rocket risk. (Times of Israel; Haaretz)

disaster_insurance.notes: A state Property Tax Compensation Fund covers war damage, against Dead Sea Transform seismic risk. (state scheme; USGS)

summary: A world-class high-tech economy with a credible central bank, sound banks, credible statistics, and an unusually young, growing population for a developed country. The drags are an eroding rule-of-law trajectory (the judicial-overhaul crisis, bills subordinating legal advisers, the prime minister's corruption trial), war-widened deficits, deep social cleavages and war trauma, and a high-variance multi-front security picture - past its peak after 2026 ceasefires but with real relapse risk - alongside growing international isolation. The Law of Return makes citizenship near-automatic for Jewish immigrants while the regime is restrictive for others.
